Name: MiniPACS
//: 7.6GB RAM host + SSD — use RAM heavily to avoid even SSD round-trips. 1.5GB SQLite page cache holds the entire ~640MB index plus headroom.
//-mmap-: mmap the SQLite index so OS page cache serves it zero-copy. 2GB covers index + attachment metadata table on SSD.
//-http-threads-: Bumped from 10 -> 50. OHIF open fires ~150 parallel HTTP requests (metadata + frames). 10 threads serialized them; 50 fits comfortably in 7.6GB RAM (~2MB stack each).
//-access-: Never was too aggressive — OHIF per-series metadata with RequestedTags silently hung because Orthanc refused to touch disk to resolve tags, and CF Tunnel cut the connection at 100s. Answers mode allows disk read only when assembling the final answer, not during the find phase.
//-extra-tags-: OHIF viewer needs these tags at query time. Without them Orthanc has to open DICOM files from disk which takes >100s for multi-series studies and trips Cloudflare Tunnel's 100s edge timeout (HTTP 524). Running /tools/reconstruct once after adding these makes every subsequent metadata call SQLite-only.
//-ohif-: OHIF plugin is now THE viewer at /ohif/ (custom bundle removed). dicom-json datasource reads the precomputed JSON attachment that the plugin generates OnStableStudy — zero disk I/O on open. Preload=true makes the SPA eagerly fetch metadata on route mount so opening feels instant. UserConfiguration overrides the bundled app-config.js — bumps web workers to 8 (matches core count) and restores MiniPACS branding.
//-lua-: Prewarm Lua DISABLED (2026-04-22). Stone Web Viewer — default viewer — does NOT read the OHIF dicom-json attachment or the full-study DICOMweb metadata. It fetches per-series metadata on demand via QIDO/WADO, so prewarm warmed cache that nobody used while burning Orthanc CPU + blocking HTTP (sync Lua callback holds Orthanc's critical section). Reactivate only if OHIF fallback usage grows — and then rewrite via Python plugin + async job queue (see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-22-minipacs-rocket-design.md Phase 2).
//-python-: Thumbnail pre-generator — OnChange(STABLE_STUDY) pushes into a bounded queue, worker thread drains at 2/sec and writes PNG to /var/cache/minipacs-thumbs (shared volume). Backfill scans the whole archive at plugin boot so old studies catch up too. Lessons from prewarm Lua baked in: non-blocking callback, separate worker, hard rate limit, backend on-demand fallback path intact.
//-stone-: Stone Web Viewer — the default portal viewer (fast cold-open, ~1MB WASM). SkipSeriesFromModalities excludes non-image SOPs that Stone's DicomResourcesLoader can't render — SEG/PR by default (segmentation masks, presentation states), plus SR/OT/KO/DOC that otherwise surface as 'OrthancException on callable: Not implemented yet'. InfoPanel/Disclaimer off for branded use — we render this as MiniPACS, not as the upstream Stone demo. InstitutionLogo overrides the Orthanc logo in the viewer's corner with our favicon (same-origin, served by the frontend nginx).
//-postgresql-: PostgreSQL holds the Orthanc index (replaces SQLite's global write lock with row-level locks — real concurrent C-STORE from multiple modalities). EnableStorage=false keeps DICOM blobs on disk (simpler backup, no PG bloat). Connection settings come from POSTGRESQL_* env vars in docker-compose; this block only turns the plugin on and sizes the connection pool. Lock=false because this is a single-instance deployment — an advisory lock adds nothing and causes Orthanc to exit whenever the PG connection blips. IndexConnectionsCount=25 sized for HttpThreadsCount=50 + ConcurrentJobs=6 + burst DICOMweb metadata — the previous 10 serialized under moderate load.

DicomWeb.//-studies-: OHIF issue #1680 — viewer hangs with MainDicomTags here, requires Full. Study-level metadata is fetched once per study open, so the disk read cost is bounded.
DicomWeb.//-series-: Series metadata served from the SQLite index — no file reads. Per-series request at 295-instance MR was 22s with 'Full' because Orthanc opened every DICOM file; MainDicomTags makes it ~50ms.
DicomWeb.//-cache-: Cache the computed metadata list per series. 30000 × ~10KB ≈ 300MB — holds every series in the archive with headroom. Warm second-open returns from RAM.
DicomWeb.//-transcode-: On-the-fly transcode to JPEG 2000 Lossless (1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.90) when WADO-RS clients don't pin a transfer syntax. Mathematically lossless; cuts wire bytes ~40–50% vs Explicit VR Little Endian on CT/MR. Stone Web Viewer accepts it via OpenJPEG.
